title: ChatPresenceEvent double-option fields should use Patch<String>
description: ChatPresenceEvent.status_text and status_emoji are typed Option<Option<String>> to represent three states: absent (no change), Some(None) (clear), Some(Some(s)) (set to s). This double-option pattern is hard to use and opaque: code must write if let Some(update) = event.status_text { if let Some(text) = update { ... } else { /* clear */ } }. The crate already has Patch<T> { Keep, Set(T), Clear } which represents exactly this three-way semantics. Using Patch<String> for these fields would be consistent with how nullable-update fields are represented everywhere else in the API (MessagePatch, ChatPatch, etc.). Fix: change status_text and status_emoji in ChatPresenceEvent to Patch<String> and implement a serde deserializer for Patch<String> that handles absent/null/string correctly.
